stories.headline: Trump touts 'great settlement' with Iran and a weekend signing in Europe to reopen Hormuz; Tehran disputes any final deal
stories.summary: President Trump said the US had 'made a great settlement of the war' with Iran and that a signing could come over the weekend in Europe, with the Strait of Hormuz reopening once a memorandum is finalized. Iran's Foreign Ministry said no final agreement had been concluded and that the strait remained under regional management, directly disputing Trump's framing. The US naval blockade stays in force pending any signing.

perspectives.keyFraming: Washington frames an imminent deal that reopens Hormuz, while the terms remain unsigned and contested.
perspectives.summary: Reuters and CNN reported Trump said the war was settled 'subject to finalization,' expecting a signing within days in Europe that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Officials cautioned the text was not final, and Trump said the naval blockade would remain until any agreement is signed.

sources.title: Trump says US and Iran reached a settlement; signing expected soon
Trump says 'great settlement' on Iran to be signed soon and strait will open

perspectives.keyFraming: Coverage stresses Tehran's denial that any settlement is final and skepticism about the announcement's substance.
perspectives.summary: NPR reported Trump canceled planned strikes and promised a deal 'soon,' while NBC News quoted Iranian officials saying no final decision had been reached, underscoring the gap between the US announcement and Tehran's position after weeks of strikes.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
Live updates: US, Iran, Trump deal, war, Hormuz

perspectives.keyFraming: European-facing outlets note the proposed signing venue in Europe and the unresolved status of Iran's commitments.
perspectives.summary: RFE/RL reported Trump announced a 'great settlement' to be signed 'quickly,' possibly in Europe, while details on enrichment levels, Hormuz access and sanctions relief remained unspecified and Iranian officials declined to confirm any agreement.

sources.title: Trump announces 'great settlement' reached with Iran, says to be signed 'quickly'

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel says it is not a party to the emerging deal even as it credits US assurances on Iran's nuclear program.
perspectives.summary: Israeli officials said Israel is not party to the emerging US-Iran agreement, with Prime Minister Netanyahu praising Trump's stated commitment to preventing an Iranian nuclear weapon while stressing Israel did not sign any memorandum.

sources.title: Netanyahu says Israel not party to Iran deal but praises Trump for commitment to thwarting nukes

stories.headline: Oil tumbles about 3% as Trump cancels Iran strikes and claims the war is 'settled' pending a signing
stories.summary: Crude reversed an earlier war-premium spike on June 11 after President Trump abruptly canceled strikes he had threatened hours earlier and said the US had 'made a great settlement of the war with Iran,' subject to finalization. Brent settled near $90.4 (down about 3%) and WTI near $87.7, both extending losses after-hours. Iran did not confirm a deal, and its claimed total closure of the Strait of Hormuz remained disputed by US CENTCOM, leaving a residual war premium.

perspectives.keyFraming: Traders faded the morning spike on the strike cancellation while keeping a war premium given no confirmed Iranian sign-off.
perspectives.summary: Brent swung below $93 intraday before settling down about 3% near $90.38; WTI fell about 2% to $87.71. Trump's cancellation of planned strikes and claim of a settlement 'subject to finalization' drove the reversal. Reports said tanker traffic through Hormuz had increased, easing acute supply fears even as Iran's IRGC claimed a total closure that CENTCOM disputed.

sources.title: Brent, WTI oil prices: Trump calls off Iran strikes
Trump claims Iran war settled 'subject to finalization,' expects signing in 'next few days'
Oil extends losses as Trump calls off planned strikes on Iran

perspectives.keyFraming: A durable deal reopening Hormuz could unwind the oil shock behind the worst inflation in three years — a potential economic win.
perspectives.summary: Bloomberg reported Trump said discussions had been 'brought to the highest level of Iranian leadership and approved.' Analysts noted a credible deal would restore disrupted Gulf flows and relieve pump prices into the FOMC, while skeptics flagged Iran's silence and Trump's repeated same-day reversals as reasons for caution.

sources.title: Trump Cancels Planned Strikes Against Iran in Latest Reversal

perspectives.keyFraming: Another same-day reversal underscores erratic policymaking; Iran has not confirmed any deal and the strait remains contested.
perspectives.summary: NPR and the Washington Post noted Trump went from vowing to 'hit 'em hard tonight' to canceling strikes and announcing a settlement within hours, with no Iranian confirmation and US bases freshly targeted, keeping the risk of a return to all-out war elevated even as markets chose to price in de-escalation.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes
Trump says he has canceled plans to strike Iran, claiming deal is near

stories.headline: SpaceX completes the largest IPO on record, raising about $75 billion in its Nasdaq debut
stories.summary: SpaceX raised roughly $75 billion by selling about 555.6 million shares at $135 apiece under the ticker SPCX, the biggest initial public offering on record, valuing the company near $1.77 trillion — among the most valuable US firms. The Nasdaq debut June 12 capped a closely watched listing amid otherwise choppy markets.

perspectives.keyFraming: Reported as a record-shattering listing and a milestone for private-space valuations.
perspectives.summary: CNBC reported SpaceX raised about $75 billion in a record-setting IPO ahead of its Nasdaq debut, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 for a valuation near $1.77 trillion, surpassing prior IPO records and ranking the company among the most valuable in the US.

sources.title: SpaceX raises $75 billion in record-setting IPO ahead of Nasdaq debut

perspectives.keyFraming: Business press frames the debut as a barometer of risk appetite after a volatile week.
perspectives.summary: TheStreet's market coverage framed the SpaceX listing as a test of investor appetite, noting early trade hugged the $135 offer price as the broader market steadied following the week's semiconductor-led volatility and an easing oil price.

sources.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq updates — June 12, 2026

stories.headline: Trump's Iran 'settlement' claim collides with Senate war-powers push and bipartisan demands for review
stories.summary: Trump's announcement that he had 'settled' the Iran war landed as the Senate pressed a war-powers resolution and lawmakers in both parties demanded any deal be submitted to Congress. Trump argued the war-powers clock no longer applies because hostilities have 'terminated,' while GOP hawks insisted on reviewing the terms and removing Iran's enriched uranium, setting up a constitutional clash even if the unsigned deal holds.

perspectives.keyFraming: The administration says the war-powers question is moot because hostilities have 'terminated,' setting up a procedural clash even if a deal holds.
perspectives.summary: PBS reported Trump claimed the congressional deadline to authorize the war 'doesn't apply' because hostilities had ended. Analysts note that even a passed resolution faces a likely veto, and that a fragile, unsigned settlement — with Tehran disputing the terms — leaves the underlying authorization question unresolved.

sources.title: Trump says deadline for Congress to approve Iran war doesn't apply, claiming hostilities have 'terminated'

perspectives.keyFraming: Hawks want the deal scrutinized, not the war restrained, demanding congressional review and removal of Iran's highly enriched uranium.
perspectives.summary: Fox News reported Sen. Lindsey Graham warned the emerging deal had 'troubling aspects' and should be 'presented to Congress for review and approval,' echoing the Obama-era review model and insisting Iran's enriched uranium be controlled and removed before any sanctions relief.

sources.title: Top GOP hawk Graham warns Iran deal has 'troubling aspects' as ceasefire begins

perspectives.keyFraming: Democrats want a binding vote regardless of Trump's unilateral 'settlement' claims, calling the conflict an unconstitutional war of choice.
perspectives.summary: NPR reported Sen. Tim Kaine and Leader Schumer continued pressing for a final Senate vote on the war-powers resolution, arguing a presidential ceasefire claim is no substitute for congressional authorization and that Congress must reassert its war powers even as the administration declares the fighting over.

sources.title: Trump now says a peace deal will be announced 'soon,' cancels further strikes

stories.headline: May wholesale prices jump 1.1%, lifting annual PPI to 6.5% — highest since 2022 — on the energy shock
stories.summary: The May Producer Price Index rose 1.1% month-over-month, well above expectations, pushing 12-month final-demand inflation to 6.5%, the hottest since November 2022. Roughly 80% of the increase traced to a 10.7% jump in final-demand energy, with wholesale gasoline up 23.4%, confirming the Strait of Hormuz oil shock is flowing through to producer prices. Core measures came in softer than forecast, days before the June 16-17 FOMC meeting.

perspectives.keyFraming: The oil shock is now visible in wholesale prices, though soft core suggests limited broadening so far.
perspectives.summary: BLS data showed final-demand PPI up 1.1% in May and 6.5% over the year, the highest annual wholesale inflation since November 2022, driven overwhelmingly by a 10.7% energy surge. Economists noted the pass-through from the Hormuz-driven crude spike, while a tamer core reading offered the Fed a partial cushion ahead of its June 16-17 decision.

sources.title: Producer Price Index News Release — 2026 M05 Results

perspectives.keyFraming: A second hot inflation print revives bets the Fed's next move could be a hike, not a cut.
perspectives.summary: Coverage framed the back-to-back 4.2% CPI and 6.5% PPI readings as cementing a hawkish hold at the June 16-17 meeting and reviving market-implied odds of a 2026 rate hike, with the energy-driven surge tied directly to the Iran war and the closed Strait of Hormuz.

sources.title: May Inflation Jumps To 4.2%, Highest Since 2023, Fueling Fed Hike Odds
